mechanism_narrative: A sustained downward trend in the federal funds rate over the preceding 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and eases financial conditions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS slope and 20d forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-slope periods do not show statistically higher mean forward returns than negative-slope periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points relative to its trailing 12-month low (Sahm-rule style trigger), the labor market is entering deterioration, which historically anticipates earnings downgrades and recessionary repricing, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp above its trailing 12-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=4.0 as of 2025-01-01 vintage, near multi-year lows but rising from 2023 trough
regime_hint: labor market turning point / early recession signal
failure_condition: In triggered instances, S&P 500 60d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or mean triggered-period return is not significantly below unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) indicate a broad-based consumer and housing demand slowdown that individually weak single-series signals do not capture, jointly predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM < 0 AND HOUST MoM < 0 concurrently
failure_condition: Joint-decline instances produce 20d forward returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional distribution (p > 0.05), or the joint signal underperforms either single-series signal alone in out-of-sample Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Positive year-over-year INDPRO growth signals healthy industrial output and supports corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days, but this relationship holds only during late-cycle expansions when capacity utilization is rising rather than during early recovery or contraction phases.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY > 0 while GDPC1 YoY growth positive for 2+ consecutive quarters
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value=103.511 revised_value=100.0647 showing revision uncertainty
failure_condition: During late-cycle expansion regimes as identified by positive GDPC1 growth, positive INDPRO YoY periods fail to produce significantly higher mean 60d forward returns than negative INDPRO YoY periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in PAYEMS reflects continued labor income growth and consumer spending capacity, which supports near-term equity momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days on an always-on basis.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month PAYEMS growth rate and 5d forward returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, indicating no exploitable short-horizon relationship.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 3%, markets price in a higher probability of continued Fed tightening or delayed easing, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns during CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% periods are not significantly lower than the unconditional mean (p > 0.05), or more than 55% of such periods show positive forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Restrictive monetary policy (elevated FEDFUNDS) combined with decelerating real GDP growth (GDPC1) jointly signals a demand contraction risk that a single indicator alone does not capture, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days because tightening amplifies the growth slowdown's impact on equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% AND GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 1.5%
failure_condition: Joint trigger instances show 60d forward returns not significantly different from unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or the interaction signal's Rank IC does not exceed the best single-series signal by more than 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in INDPRO occurring while FEDFUNDS is above 4% (a tightening regime) signals that monetary restriction is now actively curbing industrial output, foreshadowing earnings weakness and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days; the same INDPRO decline in a low-rate regime does not carry the same signal.
failure_condition: Within tightening regimes, INDPRO MoM decline instances fail to produce significantly negative 5d forward returns relative to baseline (p > 0.05), or the effect disappears when tested across multiple tightening episodes (2006-07, 2018-19, 2022-23).
